title: maerua triphylla var. calophylla
intro: Maerua triphylla var. calophylla, also known as the Bitter Apple, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its bitter fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating infections, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Maerua triphylla var. calophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. The plant is often prepared for herbal use by making a decoction from its roots and leaves, which can be administered as a tea or used topically for wound care and skin conditions.
